source_elements.indication: type 2 diabetes mellitus with inadequate glycemic control on metformin monotherapy

gold_prose: The primary efficacy endpoint is the change from baseline in glycated hemoglobin (HbA1c) at Week 24. HbA1c is a well-validated measure of glycemic control and is the primary endpoint recommended by FDA and EMA guidance for clinical trials in type 2 diabetes mellitus. Assessment will be performed by central laboratory analysis of venous blood samples collected at the scheduled study visits.
notes: Standard T2DM endpoint — low clinical judgment required

source_elements.indication: type 2 diabetes mellitus with inadequate glycemic control on metformin monotherapy

gold_prose: This is a Phase 2, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel-group study evaluating the efficacy and safety of STR-4021 in adult patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus with inadequate glycemic control on metformin monotherapy. The study comprises a 4-week screening period, a 24-week double-blind treatment period, and a 4-week safety follow-up period, for a total study duration of approximately 32 weeks.
notes: Structured design section — nearly deterministic from source data

source_elements.statistical_analysis_primary: MMRM analysis of change from baseline in HbA1c

gold_prose: The primary efficacy analysis will be performed on the Full Analysis Set (FAS) using a Mixed Model for Repeated Measures (MMRM) approach. The model will include change from baseline in HbA1c as the dependent variable, with treatment group, visit, treatment-by-visit interaction, baseline HbA1c, and baseline HbA1c-by-visit interaction as fixed effects. An unstructured covariance matrix will be used to model the within-patient correlation. The Kenward-Roger approximation will be used for denominator degrees of freedom.
notes: Requires correct statistical terminology and MMRM specification conventions

gold_prose: Eligible participants are adults aged 18 to 75 years with a confirmed diagnosis of type 2 diabetes mellitus. Key inclusion criteria include: HbA1c between 7.5% and 10.5% at screening, and stable metformin monotherapy at a dose of at least 1000 mg/day for a minimum of 3 months prior to screening. Patients are excluded if they have type 1 diabetes mellitus or an eGFR below 45 mL/min/1.73m² at screening.
notes: Eligibility criteria — factual, no characterization required
